description_long: This is a traditional hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a large, single-edged steel blade. The handle displays a natural wood grain with a smooth finish, tapering slightly towards the end for improved grip and control. The blade is broad and rectangular with a gently curved cutting edge, and the poll is flat, suitable for striking. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a wedged fit. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or branding visible. The axe shows signs of practical design, likely intended for chopping wood or similar tasks in urban or suburban environments.
description: A traditional hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, single-edged steel blade, designed for chopping tasks. The finish is utilitarian, with visible wood grain and a sturdy, wedged blade mount.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow profile of the axe head and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, rectangular blade with a curved edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the axe head and the handle's taper, with the poll of the blade visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other face of the blade and the handle's curvature, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth finish, oval cross-section, and ergonomic grip; traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, rectangular steel blade with a single curved cutting edge and flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fit with the blade head mounted onto the wooden handle.
